title: Chronicle S2: deterministic token-capped recent-outcomes digest writer + chronicle policy block
summary: BASE PACKAGE UPDATE 2026-07-11: implement in go/internal/recurrence (cycle 661 landed the ledger there; the planned internal/chronicle name is retired - never-duplicate). recurrence.WriteDigest(workspacePath string, in DigestInput, cfg DigestConfig) error -> recent-outcomes.md. DigestInput{Dossiers []dossier.Dossier, FailedApproaches []failureadapter.Entry, Index chronicle.Index} assembled by the CALLER (keeps WriteDigest pure). Rendering: newest-first, one line per cycle {cycle, task/goal, verdict, one-line why, carryover ids}, every line passed through a sanitizeLine (the triage.go sanitizePromptValue idiom - collapse \n\r\t; lesson text is LLM-authored and enters prompts = injection channel), truncation at cfg.TokenBudget via LOCAL len/4 estimator (comment cites deliverable.charsPerToken; do NOT import deliverable - core cycle), top-K NON-generic PatternStats section, generic patterns roll up to ONE aggregate line ('158 floor lessons: operator-reset x96, loop-fatal x62'). ALSO this slice: policy.ChroniclePolicy/ChronicleConfig() in NEW FILE go/internal/policy/policy_chronicle.go (policy.go is 1522 lines) mirroring GatesPolicy: compiled defaults digest=shadow, digest_tokens=1200, digest_cycles=10, escalation=shadow, historian=off; absent block => defaults; NO feature flags. Bump step 0.03 / cap 0.99 / autofile weight 0.8 / historian cap 3 stay compiled constants.
acceptance: RED: TestWriteDigest_SanitizesLessonTextControlChars (a lesson with embedded newline/bullet forgery renders as one sanitized line)
go test -race PASS; apicover clean (new exported symbols named)
